task_completion.description: Verify the script reads buoy_41046_2022.txt and filters for September data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the script's code. It should open 'buoy_41046_2022.txt' and have a condition checking if the month column (MM) equals 9 or '09'.

task_completion.description: Verify the script ignores missing values '99.00' for wave height and '9999.0' for pressure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the script's code. It should have conditions to exclude, filter out, or ignore values equal to 99.00 (or 99) for WVHT and 9999.0 (or 9999) for PRES.

task_completion.description: Verify that hurricane_summary.txt is created in /home/ga/Documents/.

task_completion.description: Verify the summary file contains the exact required text format for wave height and pressure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the contents of 'hurricane_summary.txt' (e.g., via 'cat' in the terminal or open in an editor). It must contain exactly 'Maximum Wave Height: [Value] m' and 'Minimum Pressure: [Value] hPa' with numerical values in place of the brackets.

integrity.description: Verify the script calculates the results dynamically rather than hardcoding the final answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the script code to ensure it computes the max and min from the dataset variables rather than just printing hardcoded numbers to the output file.

integrity.description: Verify the script was actually executed to produce the output.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for terminal output showing the execution of the script (e.g., 'python3 analyze_hurricane.py' or './analyze_hurricane.py').

integrity.description: Verify the output is saved to the actual file, not just printed to the console.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence that 'hurricane_summary.txt' exists as a file on disk, such as a 'cat' command displaying its contents or the file being open in a text editor, rather than just seeing the output in the standard terminal stdout.
